body:
This creates complexity and leaks details all over the codebase that shouldn't be leaked. Also, it breaks collaboration (latest value is not synced to remote clients while editing), and may break other things (now or in the future). Lastly, it's not clear from the code what this does at all, so would require extensive comments and such.

In any case, we can just keep the original text as a local state within the wysiwyg editor closure, no?

body: isn't this the analogue for what we're doing on submit if not collaborating? If so, we're computing much more (and differently) on submit?
author: dwelle

body: Also, if this should work when collaborating (and it presumably doesn't affect perf), then why not do it all the time and reduce complexity (having to keep two separate implems)?
author: dwelle

body: Yes this things are same what we do on submit but the reason I am not doing it all time and specifically when `collab/submit` is I am worried about perf since `wrapText` is called on every change and mutation for coords will also happen on every change so If it affects perf in any way(Testing looks fine though) hence avoiding rn.
author: ad1992

body:
I think it's a good idea to align the implems for 2 reasons: 

1. Complexity
2. Generally, perf is worse when collaborating. And it's much more likely we'll notice perf issues locally, thus more likely we will do something about it, rather than if it's hidden under collab.

(2) actually relates to everything, like noticing there are bugs in the collab implem only.
author: dwelle

body: Also, in E+ we're collaborating pretty much all the time, anyway.
author: dwelle

body: Hmm makes sense, I have aligned the implementation to be same irrespective of collab/submit

This kinda kills performance when there are lots of links on the screen. Can we render this onto the cached canvas same as we do with elements themselves?

AFAIK It'd require to do 2 things:

1. account for the increased dimensions of the canvas in the top-right corner
2. invalidate the cache on selection/deselection since we'll need to rerender with/without the icon

body:
by my original comment I meant redraw the text so it's has the bounding box as if it's not bound (which is this PRs purpose) so that there's no difference between the state after unbinding and when you go edit the text afterward.

If we use the `originalText` instead, what we'll end up with is somewhat of an invalid state: you can't get to it currently (until we implement #4267, and even then it'll work slightly differently as we'll need to tell the element that it's bounded), and also we'll persist hard line-breaks which means users couldn't remove them other than manually editing them out.

body:
let's distinguish between an `update` and `edit`. In here we're tracking when a user clicks on the edit button, but doesn't necessarily update. 

I didn't add a separate `update` event as I don't think it's useful knowledge. Technically even the edit isn't, if we don't distinguish how the user edits (i.e. which button they used), so we wouldn't be able to figure out which UI is being used more often.

We should prob remove this altogether or track the actual UI button.
author: dwelle

body: IMO not useful to know, especially if we don't track how the popup was closed.

body: @dwelle: returning from `onGestureChange` if `gesture.pointers.size > 2 && penMode && isExcalidrawElement` results in a smooth drawing experience. If I try really hard, I can produce unwanted zoom and jump, but even deliberately trying it is hard... I need to slide my hand onto the screen before starting to draw, to achieve the jump. This way two-finger pinch-zoom and panning both works even in penMode!

Having pinch zoom during freedraw is very valuable. you can zoom in to work on small detail, and zoom out for bigger strokes. Needing to change tools breaks the flow.

author: zsviczian

body:
Now it works... but I simply removed check for isMobile || isIPad.
It turns out `export const isIPad = /iPad/.test(window.navigator.platform);` resolves to false on iOS 15.1 (in my case).
I see no negative effect of removing the check for mobile and iPad and allowing link click on desktop as well. Why was it limited to isMobile?
author: zsviczian

body: @dwelle, btw, my finding above also suggests that probably isDarwin resolves to false on iOS15.1 as well. Back to the conversation from yesterday, these device checks are flimsy. Maybe would be better to use a package like [platform.js](https://www.npmjs.com/package/platform) - thought this one seems to be dead, it was last updated two years ago.
author: zsviczian

body:
> I see no negative effect of removing the check for mobile and iPad and allowing link click on desktop as well. Why was it limited to isMobile?

Yes, link-click can have the threshold on desktop as well, but on the link icon only (not the element body — which on Mobile is disabled, anyway)

> Maybe would be better to use a package like [platform.js](https://www.npmjs.com/package/platform) - thought this one seems to be dead, it was last updated two years ago.

I'm not sure, and as you said, it's not up-to-date. It purportedly [detects iPad as Mac](https://github.com/bestiejs/platform.js/issues/194).
author: dwelle

body:
@ad1992, why are we checking `isMobile` in `isPointHittingLinkIcon`? Is that really necessary. With the last change I practically disabled that check. I modified isIPad to `isIPad = true` and pass `this.isMobile || isPad` to isPointHittingLinkIcon in every case.... and I can't spot any negative effect on the desktop.

Thus my recommendation for this PR is to remove isMobile from `isPointHittingLinkIcon` and to remove the check for isMobile from `private handleCanvasPointerUp`.
author: zsviczian

body: The `isMobile` check in `isPointHittingLinkIcon` was added to make sure that clicking on element takes to link only in view mode in non mobile devices, this means that for `ipad` also in view mode the element will be clickable.
author: ad1992

body: why setting it to true ?
author: ad1992

body: this would mean that in view mode in ipad the element is not clickable, is that something you want ?
author: ad1992

body:
Based on some testing, my answer is, yes, however at this time isIPad is not reliable.
I think instead of isMobile and isIPad we should have something like isTouchScreen or similar. I would detect it similar to penMode.
author: zsviczian

body: See my comment above. I set this to true because I had a hunch that it does not resolve correctly for iOS15.1 (and my hunch proved to be correct. Setting this to fixed true the links started to work on my iOS15.1 iPad as well.
author: zsviczian

body:
as discussed, changed my mind — multi-element resizing right now is maintaining aspect ratio, so it should maintain text-bound containers' aspect ratio as well, hence scale the text instead of reflow.

Once we make multi-element resizing non-proportional, we can revert this behavior (and keep it under `Shift` modifier).

body:
> should we be putting source (ui/keyboard/contextmenu...) as label? label in ga should generally be used to specify/qualify the action. So let's say if we wanted to track which strokeStyle value user picked, we'd most likely wanna put it in label (ga also has value attribute, but that's numeric only so not useful for most of our properties). Currently we don't track property values.

`label` might not be the right place for `source` how instead we add `custom dimension`  `source` and use that here?

> should we distinguish between mobile ui and desktop ui (not talking about device, but our UI breakpoints)?

Yeah, let's track the breakpoints as well.
